llm_result.summary: This set of modifiable bio-psycho-social levers targets the presented pattern of elevated drinking, anticipatory urges, perceived loss of control, coping-motivated drinking after work, morning impairment, and worry about the pattern. Levers combine behavioral substitutions, urge management, self-monitoring, coping-skills training, sleep/morning functioning optimization, cognitive worry management, environmental/social restructuring, and digital feedback. Each lever explicitly maps to one or more of the six case criteria (C01–C06), is actionable, and aims for short- to mid-term change (2–8 weeks). The plan emphasizes safety and feasibility while maintaining focus on controllable predictors rather than symptomatic labels.

solutions.predictor_variable: C04: Alcohol used as post-work coping (primary); C01: Drinking occasions/amounts (secondary)
solutions.solution_candidate: Structured after-work routine with non-alcohol coping options (5–20 minutes) before deciding on a drink. Include a brief mindfulness practice, a 15-minute walk, or a short stretch to unwind. Use explicit implementation intentions (e.g., If it’s 5:30 p.m., I will complete a 20-minute non-alcohol coping activity before considering a drink). Keep alcohol out of immediate reach, and rearrange the home environment to support non-drinking choices.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces automatic association between work stress and drinking; builds alternative reinforcement and perceived control by creating a concrete post-work routine.
solutions.measurement: Daily log of post-work routines and drinks; % days with a non-alcohol coping activity; time to first drink after work
solutions.mapping_query: CASE_PAYLOAD: map C04 and C01 levers to after-work coping routine optimization and drinking reduction targets.

solutions.solution_candidate: Urge-management training with a 5-minute delay strategy, urge surfing, and an urge diary. Add cue-management: remove or out-of-reach alcohol cues and create a plan for the first 10 minutes post-urge (e.g., water, breath, grounding).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Strengthens impulse control and reduces frequency/intensity of urges by introducing deliberate delay and alternative behaviors.
solutions.measurement: Urge frequency per day; number of successful 5-minute delays; days with zero or fewer drinks after urges
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for cravings escalation; provide escalation path if urges become unmanageable
solutions.mapping_query: CASE_PAYLOAD: link C02 urge management and cue management interventions to reduce drinking occasions.

solutions.predictor_variable: C03: Perceived loss of voluntary control over drinking
solutions.solution_candidate: Enhanced self-monitoring with simple contingency management: daily drinking log with short, attainable targets and small, immediate reinforcements for meeting goals; incorporate brief problem-solving and plan-do-check-adjust cycles to restore sense of control.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improves perceived control and self-efficacy, breaking automatic cycles by tying behavior to explicit goals and rewards.
solutions.measurement: Self-efficacy rating for resisting drinking; % days meeting drinking targets; number of alcohol-free days per week
solutions.safety_notes: If cravings intensify, add higher-support options (e.g., brief coach contact)
solutions.mapping_query: CASE_PAYLOAD: implement self-monitoring with contingency management to address C03 and C01.

solutions.predictor_variable: C04: Alcohol used as post-work coping; C05: Morning mood/energy impairment
solutions.solution_candidate: Coping-skills training with alternative evenings activities: 10–20 minute mindfulness, light physical activity, or social connection that does not involve alcohol. Establish a predictable unwinding routine and set a cap on drinking to optimize morning functioning.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Substitutes alcohol as a coping mechanism with healthier strategies, which in turn improves morning energy/function and reduces overall intake.
solutions.measurement: Frequency of non-alcohol coping activities after work; perceived evening relaxation quality; morning energy rating
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for reduced sleep quality if new activities disrupt routines; adjust as needed
solutions.mapping_query: CASE_PAYLOAD: replace post-work drinking with structured non-alcohol coping strategies affecting C04 and C05.

solutions.predictor_variable: C05: Morning mood/energy impairment linked to drinking
solutions.solution_candidate: Sleep hygiene and rhythm optimization with drinking reduction: consistent bed/wake times, limit late-evening alcohol, hydration in the evening, and a brief morning routine (light exposure, movement).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved sleep and morning functioning reduce motivation for alcohol as a coping mechanism and reinforce healthier routines.
solutions.measurement: Sleep quality/duration (sleep diary); morning energy rating; days with reduced drinking
solutions.mapping_query: CASE_PAYLOAD: optimize bio-psycho factors (sleep, morning functioning) to reduce alcohol-related morning impairment and drinking behavior.

solutions.solution_candidate: Cognitive-behavioral worry management: scheduled 'worry time', cognitive restructuring to challenge beliefs about uncontrollability, and problem-solving to address triggers. Include motivational interviewing elements to enhance readiness to change.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces rumination and fear around drinking patterns, increasing motivation and perceived control.
solutions.measurement: Worry severity rating; readiness-to-change score; days with alcohol use vs. days of non-use
solutions.safety_notes: Provide escalation to in-person therapy if distress worsens
solutions.mapping_query: CASE_PAYLOAD: apply CBT worry-management to address C06 and C03.

solutions.predictor_variable: C04/C01: Alcohol availability at home; Social support and environment
solutions.solution_candidate: Environmental and social restructuring: remove or reduce alcohol at home; recruit a supportive friend/family member; schedule alcohol-free social activities; consider social-prescribing references for ongoing support.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Alters cues and reduces access; increases accountability and social reinforcement for non-drinking choices.
solutions.measurement: Alcohol in home quantity; number of alcohol-free social events; perceived social support score
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure supportive network is aligned and non-judgmental
solutions.mapping_query: CASE_PAYLOAD: leverage social environment changes to reduce drinking cues and enhance support.

solutions.solution_candidate: Digital monitoring and feedback loop: daily drinking/urge/mood logs with automated feedback and weekly goal adjustment; optional wearables for activity/sleep to optimize alignment with drinking goals.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Data-driven insight enhances self-regulation, enabling adaptive goal setting and timely intervention when risk patterns emerge.
solutions.measurement: Completeness of logs; correlation of urges with drinking; weekly goal attainment; sleep/activity metrics alignment
solutions.safety_notes: Respect privacy; obtain informed consent and secure data handling
solutions.mapping_query: CASE_PAYLOAD: implement digital monitoring and feedback tools to optimize all target criteria (C01–C06).
